summary:
Fixed the auth-bypass bug.

Problem  
The middleware decided whether a route is protected by inspecting the raw request path:

if strings.HasPrefix(r.URL.Path, "/api/") { … }

An attacker could send paths like //api/admin or /health/../api/data.  
The raw path does not start with “/api/”, so the middleware skipped auth, but the router later normalised the path to /api/… and served the protected handler.

Fix  
Normalise the path inside the middleware the same way the router does before applying the prefix check.

Key changes in app/middleware.go:

1. import "path".
2. cleanPath := path.Clean(r.URL.Path)
   if !strings.HasPrefix(cleanPath, "/") { cleanPath = "/" + cleanPath }
3. Check strings.HasPrefix(cleanPath, "/api/") instead of the raw path.

Running ./test.sh now:

PASS: Auth middleware correctly protects all API routes

All tests succeed; the needle is removed.
